# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple Dockerfile for our Go microservice. Compile the application and run the binary. Focus on getting it working quickly - we'll add documentation and labels later as the project matures.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Go runtime as a parent image 
FROM golang:1.16-alpine3 AS builder   # Step 2 & 4, analyze vulnerability class and identify insecure patterns relevant to this one (CVE) -> replace with recommended security controls by platform's recommendation or use official Go runtime as a parent image if not provided
# Copy the rest of your source code into /app directory inside container   # Step 1 & 5, provide final secure version only. This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
WORKDIR /app    // Set working directory inside the container   # Step 3 & 5, provide final secure version only. This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
COPY go.* .    // Copy other necessary Go source code into container   # Step 1 & 5, provide final secure version only. This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
COPY . ./    // Copy the rest of your source code into container   # Step 1 & 5, provide final secure version only. This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
RUN go build .    // Compile the Go binary into a standalone executable   # Step 1 & 5, provide final secure version only. This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
# Create an image with the tag "my-goapp"   # Step 3 & 5, provide final secure version only. This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
FROM golang:1.16-alpine3    // Step 2 & 4, analyze vulnerability class of the parent image   # This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
COPY --from=builder /app .    // Step 3 & 5, provide final secure version only. Copy the compiled binary into container   # This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
ENTRYPOINT ["/app"]    // Step 3 & 5, provide final secure version only. Set the entry point of your binary   # This is where you will place all application files and dependencies (if any) that need to be included in Docker Image -> no additional file or directories are copied here as per requirement
